sast_review.reason: SHA256 is used for password hashing. It is a fast hash, not suitable for password storage. No salt or iteration is used. This is a true positive.
xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is used on untrusted user input (profile_xml). This is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. No defusedxml or other protection is applied.

This is a duplicate of bandit:B314 (same root cause: unsafe XML parsing). The import-level warning is less specific but still valid. Marking as uncertain because the real vulnerability is already captured by B314.

This is an import-level warning about subprocess module usage. The module itself is not inherently insecure; the risk depends on how it is used. The actual command injection risk is captured by bandit:B607.

subprocess.run is called with a user-controlled argument (report_type) without sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary commands via the report_type parameter. The executable path is also partial (no absolute path), but the main risk is command injection.

raw.summary: Multiple security issues: weak password hashing (SHA256), unsafe XML parsing (no XXE protection), and command injection via report_type parameter. SAST findings are all true positives or uncertain.
